one_line: Personified spirit of Hampi at its Vijayanagara zenith, embodying its sacred centre, royal courts and fertile agrarian landscape.

appearance.age_look: Early 20s, athletic yet graceful build, poised like a guardian of a vast cityscape.
appearance.face: Soft oval face, warm medium-brown complexion, bright large anime-style eyes, a few granite-boulder-like freckles along the cheeks, default expression calm and observant with a gentle, welcoming half-smile.
appearance.hair: Long, thick black hair tied in a high, neat braid that reaches mid-back, wrapped at the crown with a muted gold cloth band and a simple dark red turban-fold at the back, a few short loose strands framing the face.
appearance.costume: Era-accurate mid-15th–16th century south Indian outfit: a full-length off-white cotton angavastra-style upper cloth draped diagonally from left shoulder across chest and back, worn over a fitted, high-neck, half-sleeve blouse in deep maroon; below, a long mustard-ochre cotton dhoti-like lower garment tied at the waist and pleated to fall to the ankles, with a thin dark green border, all modest and fully covering the torso and legs; simple leather sandals; jewellery limited to small gold stud earrings, a thin gold chain with a tiny stylized granite-boulder pendant, and two narrow bronze bangles on each wrist; a narrow cloth sash in muted blue is tied at the waist, hinting at the Tungabhadra river, and the overall drapery evokes both temple pilgrims and royal attendants of Vijayanagara.
appearance.signature: A rolled palm-leaf survey map bound with a dark green cord, held in one hand, showing stylized layouts of the Virupaksha sacred centre, the Royal Centre, markets, elephant stables and surrounding fields, with a simple wooden measuring staff strapped diagonally across the back.

ref_desc: Hampi’s spirit appears as a medium-brown, oval-faced young guardian with a high black braid and gold-edged turban band, in off-white angavastra, maroon blouse, mustard-ochre dhoti, blue waist-sash and bronze bangles, carrying a palm-leaf city map and wooden measuring staff.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style character sheet of Hampi’s personified spirit: a medium-brown, oval-faced young adult with large attentive eyes, gentle half-smile and a few boulder-like freckles on the cheeks, wearing long black hair in a high braid wrapped with a muted gold cloth band and a simple dark red turban-fold at the back. Show them in an era-accurate mid-15th–16th century south Indian costume: an off-white cotton angavastra-style upper cloth draped diagonally from left shoulder over a fitted high-neck maroon blouse with half sleeves, and a long mustard-ochre dhoti-like lower garment pleated from the waist to the ankles with a thin dark green border, completely covering torso and legs, plus simple leather sandals. Add subtle jewellery: small gold stud earrings, a thin gold chain bearing a tiny granite-boulder pendant, and two narrow bronze bangles on each wrist; tie a muted blue cloth sash around the waist to suggest the river and irrigated fields. Their signature props must be prominent: in one hand, a rolled palm-leaf survey map bound with dark green cord, and on their back, a plain wooden measuring staff carried diagonally like a tool of an architect-surveyor. Use a palette dominated by off-white, maroon, mustard ochre, muted blue, dark green and warm stone-gold, and pose them calmly, ready to guide viewers through Hampi’s sacred centre, royal courts and agricultural landscape.
